Overview Quick Start Interactive Samples Root Schema example extensions link metadata presentation purpose rich-text status component pattern style theme token accessibility anatomy api best-practice content design-specifications guideline interaction motion principle scale state variant

2 definitions in this file:

  • interactionEntry
  • interactions
 What happens during this step — the system's response, visual changes, state transitions, and any user-facing feedback. CommonMark supported. What initiates this interaction step. Describes the user action, system event, or condition that causes this step to occur (e.g., 'User submits the form by activating the submit button', 'API returns a 422 response', 'Client-side validation detects one or more invalid fields'). When omitted, the step is a continuation of the previous step's flow without a new trigger. The names of the components involved in this interaction step. Each name MUST match the `name` of a component documented elsewhere in the system. This creates a cross-reference between the pattern's interaction flow and the individual component documentation. Visual or interactive examples illustrating this specific interaction step — screenshots of the state after this step, screen recordings of the transition, or code snippets showing the implementation. References: richText, example Identifies this guideline as an interactions specification. The interaction steps in chronological order. Each item documents a single step in the flow with its trigger, description, involved components, and optional examples. The ordering is significant — tools SHOULD preserve and display steps in this order to represent the temporal sequence of the interaction.
Min items: 1 References: interactionEntry

Design System Documentation Standard (DSDS) 0.1 — Draft Specification

GitHub
